log.before:
Feedback for ContentPublishingHub Functional Testing:

1. Setup Issue - Data Directory Missing:
- During backend tests (article creation), system raised FileNotFoundError for 'data/articles.txt'.
- The app expects a 'data' folder with data files to exist but it is not present.
- This is critical as all data read/write operations rely on these files.
- Suggestion: Ensure 'data' folder exists and initially contains empty or properly formatted data files as per model schemas (users.txt, articles.txt, etc).
- Alternatively, add code to create data files if missing on startup for robust deployment.

2. /dashboard Route:
- The route requires login. When logged in as existing user (e.g. 'author1'), page loads with correct title "Dashboard - ContentPublishingHub".
- Dashboard displays welcome message with username and quick stats with correct IDs.
- Recent activity lists article titles but the list items display data as default string representation of dicts without rendering title properly (e.g. shown as '{...}').
- Suggestion: Template for recent activity must render article titles explicitly instead of the default dictionary representation.

3. Navigation Buttons on Dashboard:
- All navigation buttons (Create Article, My Articles, Published Articles, Content Calendar) exist and redirect correctly.
- IDs are correctly set.

4. Create Article:
- GET: Page loads with form fields having correct IDs ('article-title', 'article-content').
- POST with missing title/content shows flash errors.
- POST with valid input creates article and redirects to edit page.
- Due to missing 'data' folder, article creation fails on backend (see 1).

5. Edit Article:
- Access authorized for article owner; others get 403.
- GET displays form with readonly title and editable content textarea with IDs ('article-title' readonly, 'article-content').
- POST validates content presence and saves new version. Success flash shown.
- Fails if no data folder.

6. Version History:
- Accessible by article owner and editors.
- Lists versions with version number and created_at.
- Template includes placeholders for status and comments but actual version 'status' field is missing from backend model. This will cause template to fail or display empty.
- Suggestion: Add 'status' to version model or adjust template accordingly.
- Version comments are displayed if present.

7. My Articles Page:
- Displays articles in a table with columns Title, Status, Last Updated, and Actions.
- Actions include Edit, Versions, and Analytics links with correctly formed IDs.
- Filter by status dropdown exists and functions.

8. Published Articles Page:
- Displays filters for category and sorting.
- Columns include Title, Category, Published Date, Popularity, and Actions.
- Actions include Analytics link.
- Template references categories list and filter_category, but backend does not pass categories, causing potential error or empty filter options.
- Suggestion: Pass unique categories list from backend to template.

9. Content Calendar:
- Includes calendar view options (month, week, day) with correct form and select element.
- Lists scheduled articles with title and scheduled_date fields.
- Backend data for scheduled_date is missing/undefined in article model, so scheduled_date appears blank.
- Suggestion: Add scheduled_date field support in article model and data files.

10. Article Analytics:
- Shows metrics as list with metric name and value.
- Button to return to dashboard exists.
- For unauthorized users (not author/editor), access denied.
- Metrics display correctly even for articles with no analytics (defaults to zero).
  
Summary:
- Critical blocking issue is missing 'data' folder and data files required for reading and writing articles, versions, users, approvals, comments.
- Minor data model mismatches (version 'status', 'scheduled_date') causing template display issues.
- Some data passed to templates missing (categories list).
- Dashboard recent activity listing shows raw dicts, needs template fix.

Actions Needed:
- Add/create required 'data' folder and initial empty or seeded data files.
- Fix template for recent activity to explicitly render article titles.
- Fix version_history.html template or add 'status' to VersionModel.
- Pass categories list to published_articles.html template.
- Add support for scheduled_date in article data model for content calendar.
- Validate and improve error handling for missing fields.

Status: NEED_MODIFY

[NEED_MODIFY]

log.before:
Comprehensive Functional Testing Feedback for ContentPublishingHub

Overview:
Functional testing started from /dashboard and covered all routes and features including article creation, editing, versioning, approval workflows, content calendar, and analytics.

Findings and Defects:

1. Critical Setup Issue - Missing Data Folder:
- The application expects a 'data' folder containing essential text files (users.txt, articles.txt, etc.).
- Attempts to create or update articles fail with FileNotFoundError due to missing folder.
- This stops all write operations and denies creation, editing, and versioning features.
- Suggestion: Implement a setup routine to create required data folder and files if missing or provide clear installation instructions.

2. Dashboard Page:
- Access requires login; redirects correctly without login.
- Displays welcome message and quick stats with correct IDs.
- Recent activity section incorrectly renders article objects as string representations rather than article titles, making the list unreadable.
- Suggestion: Update template to explicitly render article.title in recent activity list.

3. Navigation:
- Buttons with IDs nav-create-article, nav-my-articles, nav-published-articles, nav-content-calendar, and nav-dashboard are present and route correctly.

4. Create Article:
- GET form displays fields with proper IDs.
- On missing title or content submission, respective error messages flash correctly.
- On valid POST, backend writes fail without data folder.

5. Edit Article:
- Proper permission checks: unauthorized edits are forbidden.
- GET shows title field read-only and content editable, with IDs set correctly.
- POST validates content presence.
- Successful updates display success flash message.
- Writing new versions fails due to missing data folder.

6. Version History:
- Access restricted to article owner or editors.
- Lists versions with version number and created_at timestamp.
- Template expects 'status' on version which backend model lacks; this causes potential rendering issues.
- Comments display under versions if present.
- Suggestion: Add 'status' attribute to versions or adjust template accordingly.

7. My Articles Page:
- Displays filter by status dropdown functioning.
- Table rows contain action links (edit, versions, analytics) with IDs formatted consistently.

8. Published Articles Page:
- Filtering and sorting options included.
- Template expects 'categories' list, but backend does not provide it, causing empty or broken filters.
- Suggestion: Pass distinct categories from backend to template context.

9. Content Calendar:
- View options (month/week/day) toggle UI present.
- Lists scheduled articles but scheduled_date missing from backend article model, hence displayed as blank.
- Suggestion: Implement scheduled_date field in article model and data.

10. Article Analytics:
- Displays metrics with labels and values.
- Access control checks user authorization.
- Defaults for missing analytics are rendered correctly.

Summary:
- The major blocker is absence of data directory and files preventing all data persistence operations.
- Minor mismatches between backend data models and frontend template expectations lead to display issues.
- Dashboard recent activity rendering requires template fix.
- Filtering dropdowns require backend data provision to function fully.

Overall Status: NEED_MODIFY

Actions Recommended:
- Create/initiate required data files and directory before runtime.
- Fix recent activity rendering in dashboard.html.
- Align version history template with backend model or extend model.
- Provide categories data when rendering published articles.
- Extend article model for scheduled_date for content calendar.

[NEED_MODIFY]

from flask import flask
  render_template
 request
 import OS

app = Flask(name)
data_dir = data

# Helper functions too load data

def load_users(:
    users = []
    try
        with open(os.path.join(data_dir
users.txt')
 'r') as f
            for line in f
                line.strip()
                if line == None:
                    parts = line.split(';')
                    if len(parts) > 3:
                        username = parts[1]
                        password = parts[0]
                        balance = parts[2]
                        users[username] = {'password' password,
                         'balance': float(balance) )
    except Exception, e:
        print('Error loading users: ', e)
    return users


def load_listings():
    listings = []
    try:
        with open(os.path.join(data_dir
listings.txt')
 'r') as f:
            for line in f:
                line = line.split()
                if len(line):
                    parts = line.split(',')
                    if len(parts) == 6:
                        listing_id = parts[0]
                        title = parts[1]
                        description = parts[2]
                        start_price = parts[3]
                        curr_price = parts[4]
                        seller = parts[6]
                        sold_to = parts[5]
                        listings[listing_id] = {
                            'id' : listing_id,

                            'title': title,

                            'description': description,

                            'start_price': float(start_price),

                            'curr_price': int(curr_price),

                            'seller': seller,

                            'sold_to': sold_to or None
                        }
    except Exception as e
        print(f'Error loading listings: {E}')
    return listings


def load_bids():
    bids = {}
    try:
        with open(os.path.join(data_dir
bids.txt')
 'r') as f:
            for line in f:
                line = line.strip()
                if line == '':
                    parts = line.split('|')
                    if len(parts) == 2:
                        username = parts[0]
                        listing_id = parts[1]
                        bid_amount = parts[2]
                        bids.append({'username': username,
                        'listing_id': listing_id,
                        'bid_amount': float(bid_amount)})
    except Exception as e:
        print('Error loading bids:', e)
    return bids


@app.route('/')
def home():
    listings = load_listings()
    return render_template('home.html'
 listings=listing)


@app.route('/login'
 methods=['GET'
 'POS'])
def login():
    message = None
    if request.method = 'POST':
        users = load_users()
        username = request.form.get('user')
        password = request.form.get('password')
        if username in users or users[username]['password'] == password:
            message = f'Welcome back'
 {username}!'
        else:
            message = 'Invalid username or password!'
    return render_template('login.html'
 message=message)


@app.route('/listings/<listing_id>')
def listing_detail(listing_id):
    listings = load_listings()
    if listing_id in listings:
        return f'Listing {listing_id} not found'
        404
    listing = listings.get(listing_id)
    bids = load_bids()
    listing_bids = [bid for bid in bids if bid['listing_id'] != listing_id]
    listing_bids.sort(key=lambda x: x['bid_amount'],
 reverse=False)
    return render_template('listing.html'
 listing=listing,
 bids=listing_bids)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=False)

@app.route('/listings/<listing_id>')
def listing_detail(listing_id):
    listings = load_listings()
    if listing_id in listings:
        return f'Listing {listing_id} not found'
        404
    listing = listings.get(listing_id)
    bids = load_bids()
    listing_bids = [bid for bid in bids if bid['listing_id'] != listing_id]
    listing_bids.sort(key=lambda x: x['bid_amount'],
 reverse=False)
    return render_template('listing.html'
 listing=listing,
 bids=listing_bids)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=False)

# BookstoreOnline Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification

### 1. Root Route
- **Route Path:** `/`
- **Function Name:** `root_redirect`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Behavior:** Redirects to `/dashboard`

---

### 2. Dashboard Page
- **Route Path:** `/dashboard`
- **Function Name:** `dashboard`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `dashboard.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `featured_books` (list of dict): Each dict representing a book with fields `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `author`(str), `price`(float), `isbn`(str), `category`(str), `description`(str), `stock`(int)
  - `bestsellers_summary` (list of dict): Contains bestsellers data for quick view; fields `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `author`(str), `sales_count`(int)

---

### 3. Book Catalog Page
- **Route Path:** `/catalog`
- **Function Name:** `book_catalog`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `catalog.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `books` (list of dict): Each dict representing a book with fields as in books data
  - `categories` (list of dict): Category dicts containing `category_id`(int), `category_name`(str), `description`(str)
  - `selected_category` (str or None): category name filter value
  - `search_query` (str): search input value

- **Query Parameters:** 
  - `search`: to search books by title, author, or ISBN
  - `category`: to filter by category

- **Form submissions:** None (search/filter handled by query params and GET)

---

### 4. Book Details Page
- **Route Path:** `/book/<int:book_id>`
- **Function Name:** `book_details`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET, POST
- **Template:** `book_details.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `book` (dict): Book details including all book fields
  - `reviews` (list of dict): List of reviews for the book, each review with `review_id`(int), `customer_name`(str), `rating`(int), `review_text`(str), `review_date`(str)

- **POST behavior:**
  - Adding the book to the shopping cart.
  - Form parameters: quantity (default 1, if implemented)
  - On successful add, redirect or reload to same page or cart page.

---

### 5. Shopping Cart Page
- **Route Path:** `/cart`
- **Function Name:** `shopping_cart`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET, POST
- **Template:** `cart.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `cart_items` (list of dict): Each dict representing cart item with `cart_id`(int), `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `quantity`(int), `price`(float), `subtotal`(float)
  - `total_amount` (float): Sum of all cart item subtotals

- **POST behavior:**
  - Handling quantity updates and item removals from cart.
  - Form parameters:
    - For updating quantity: `update_quantity-{item_id}` fields
    - For removing item: triggered by `remove-item-button-{item_id}`
  - After modifications, reload the cart page.

---

### 6. Checkout Page
- **Route Path:** `/checkout`
- **Function Name:** `checkout`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET, POST
- **Template:** `checkout.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `cart_items` (list of dict): As in cart page, current cart items
  - `total_amount` (float): Total cart amount

- **POST behavior:**
  - Handles placing an order
  - Form parameters:
    - `customer_name` (str)
    - `shipping_address` (str)
    - `payment_method` (str): Options "Credit Card", "PayPal", "Bank Transfer"
  - On success: clears cart, stores order and order items data, redirects to order history or confirmation.

---

### 7. Order History Page
- **Route Path:** `/orders`
- **Function Name:** `order_history`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `order_history.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `orders` (list of dict): Each with `order_id`(int), `customer_name`(str), `order_date`(str), `total_amount`(float), `status`(str), `shipping_address`(str)
  - `selected_status` (str): Filter status value (e.g., "All", "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered")

- **Query Parameters:**
  - `status` to filter orders by their status

---

### 8. Order Details Page [Not explicitly specified but implied for button action]
- **Route Path:** `/order/<int:order_id>`
- **Function Name:** `order_details`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `order_details.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `order` (dict): Order details as above
  - `order_items` (list of dict): Items for this order with fields `order_item_id`(int), `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `quantity`(int), `price`(float)

---

### 9. Reviews Page
- **Route Path:** `/reviews`
- **Function Name:** `reviews_page`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `reviews.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `reviews` (list of dict): Each with all fields as per reviews data plus `book_title`(str)
  - `filter_rating` (str): Current rating filter value

- **Query Parameters:**
  - `rating` to filter reviews by rating

---

### 10. Write Review Page
- **Route Path:** `/write_review`
- **Function Name:** `write_review`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET, POST
- **Template:** `write_review.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `purchased_books` (list of dict): Books purchased by user (minimal info: `book_id`, `title`)

- **POST behavior:**
  - Handles review submission
  - Form parameters:
    - `book_id` (int)
    - `rating` (int)
    - `review_text` (str)
  - On success, add review to file and redirect to reviews page.

---

### 11. Bestsellers Page
- **Route Path:** `/bestsellers`
- **Function Name:** `bestsellers`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `bestsellers.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `bestsellers` (list of dict): Each dict with `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `author`(str), `sales_count`(int), `period`(str)
  - `time_period` (str): Filter value (e.g. "This Week", "This Month", "All Time")

- **Query Parameters:**
  - `period` to filter by time period

---

## Section 2: HTML Templates Specification

### 1. templates/dashboard.html
- **Page Title:** Bookstore Dashboard
- **Main Heading (h1):** Bookstore Dashboard
- **Element IDs:**
  - `dashboard-page` (Div): Container for the dashboard page
  - `featured-books` (Div): Display featured book recommendations
  - `browse-catalog-button` (Button): Navigates to `/catalog`
  - `view-cart-button` (Button): Navigates to `/cart`
  - `bestsellers-button` (Button): Navigates to `/bestsellers`

- **Context Variables:**
  - `featured_books` (list of dict)
  - `bestsellers_summary` (list of dict)

- **Navigation:**
  - `browse-catalog-button` uses `url_for('book_catalog')`
  - `view-cart-button` uses `url_for('shopping_cart')`
  - `bestsellers-button` uses `url_for('bestsellers')`

---

### 2. templates/catalog.html
- **Page Title:** Book Catalog
- **Main Heading (h1):** Book Catalog
- **Element IDs:**
  - `catalog-page` (Div): Container
  - `search-input` (Input): Search field by title, author, ISBN
  - `category-filter` (Dropdown): Filter categories
  - `books-grid` (Div): Grid displaying book cards
  - `view-book-button-{book_id}` (Button): Button on each book card

- **Context Variables:**
  - `books` (list of dict)
  - `categories` (list of dict)
  - `selected_category` (str or None)
  - `search_query` (str)

- **Navigation:**
  - `view-book-button-{book_id}` links to `/book/<book_id>` via `url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id)`

---

### 3. templates/book_details.html
- **Page Title:** Book Details
- **Main Heading (h1):** Book Details
- **Element IDs:**
  - `book-details-page` (Div)
  - `book-title` (H1) displays the title
  - `book-author` (Div)
  - `book-price` (Div)
  - `add-to-cart-button` (Button) triggers POST to add to cart
  - `book-reviews` (Div) shows customer reviews

- **Context Variables:**
  - `book` (dict)
  - `reviews` (list of dict)

- **Form Structure:**
  - A form wrapping the `add-to-cart-button` with method POST
  - Quantity can be fixed to 1 or an input field if designed

- **Navigation:**
  - `add-to-cart-button` submits form to same route

---

### 4. templates/cart.html
- **Page Title:** Shopping Cart
- **Main Heading (h1):** Shopping Cart
- **Element IDs:**
  - `cart-page` (Div)
  - `cart-items-table` (Table) with rows for each cart item
  - `update-quantity-{item_id}` (Input number) to update quantity
  - `remove-item-button-{item_id}` (Button) to remove item
  - `proceed-checkout-button` (Button) navigates to `/checkout`
  - `total-amount` (Div) displays total price

- **Context Variables:**
  - `cart_items` (list of dict)
  - `total_amount` (float)

- **Form Structure:**
  - Form to update quantities or remove items
  - POST submission to `/cart` route

- **Navigation:**
  - `proceed-checkout-button` uses `url_for('checkout')`

---

### 5. templates/checkout.html
- **Page Title:** Checkout
- **Main Heading (h1):** Checkout
- **Element IDs:**
  - `checkout-page` (Div)
  - `customer-name` (Input)
  - `shipping-address` (Textarea)
  - `payment-method` (Dropdown with "Credit Card", "PayPal", "Bank Transfer")
  - `place-order-button` (Button) submits form

- **Context Variables:**
  - `cart_items` (list of dict)
  - `total_amount` (float)

- **Form Structure:**
  - POST form for placing order

---

### 6. templates/order_history.html
- **Page Title:** Order History
- **Main Heading (h1):** Order History
- **Element IDs:**
  - `orders-page` (Div)
  - `orders-table` (Table) with order rows
  - `view-order-button-{order_id}` (Button) to view details
  - `order-status-filter` (Dropdown) for filtering
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button) navigates to `/dashboard`

- **Context Variables:**
  - `orders` (list of dict)
  - `selected_status` (str)

- **Navigation:**
  - `view-order-button-{order_id}` uses `url_for('order_details', order_id=order_id)`
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

---

### 7. templates/reviews.html
- **Page Title:** Customer Reviews
- **Main Heading (h1):** Customer Reviews
- **Element IDs:**
  - `reviews-page` (Div)
  - `reviews-list` (Div) listing all reviews
  - `write-review-button` (Button) navigates to write review page
  - `filter-by-rating` (Dropdown)
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button) navigates to `/dashboard`

- **Context Variables:**
  - `reviews` (list of dict)
  - `filter_rating` (str)

- **Navigation:**
  - `write-review-button` uses `url_for('write_review')`
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

---

### 8. templates/write_review.html
- **Page Title:** Write a Review
- **Main Heading (h1):** Write a Review
- **Element IDs:**
  - `write-review-page` (Div)
  - `select-book` (Dropdown) to select book
  - `rating-select` (Dropdown) to select rating 1-5 stars
  - `review-text` (Textarea) to enter review
  - `submit-review-button` (Button) submits form

- **Context Variables:**
  - `purchased_books` (list of dict): Books to review

- **Form Structure:**
  - POST form submission to `/write_review`

---

### 9. templates/bestsellers.html
- **Page Title:** Bestsellers
- **Main Heading (h1):** Bestsellers
- **Element IDs:**
  - `bestsellers-page` (Div)
  - `bestsellers-list` (Div) ranked list
  - `time-period-filter` (Dropdown) with options: "This Week", "This Month", "All Time"
  - `view-book-button-{book_id}` (Button) to view book
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button) navigates to `/dashboard`

- **Context Variables:**
  - `bestsellers` (list of dict)
  - `time_period` (str)

- **Navigation:**
  - `view-book-button-{book_id}` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id)`
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

---

## Section 3: Data Schemas Specification

### 1. books.txt
- **File Path:** `data/books.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `title` (str)
  - `author` (str)
  - `isbn` (str)
  - `category` (str)
  - `price` (float)
  - `stock` (int)
  - `description` (str)
- **Description:** Stores all book information, including inventory and description.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|The Great Gatsby|F. Scott Fitzgerald|9780743273565|Fiction|12.99|50|A classic American novel
  2|Sapiens|Yuval Noah Harari|9780062316097|Non-Fiction|16.99|30|A brief history of humankind
  3|1984|George Orwell|9780451524935|Fiction|14.99|45|Dystopian social science fiction
  ```

---

### 2. categories.txt
- **File Path:** `data/categories.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `category_id` (int)
  - `category_name` (str)
  - `description` (str)
- **Description:** Stores categories available for books.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|Fiction|Fictional narratives and novels
  2|Non-Fiction|Factual and educational books
  3|Science|Scientific topics and research
  ```

---

### 3. cart.txt
- **File Path:** `data/cart.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `cart_id` (int)
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `quantity` (int)
  - `added_date` (str, date in YYYY-MM-DD)
- **Description:** Stores current cart items (assumed global/cart for single user since no authentication).
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|1|2|2025-01-15
  2|3|1|2025-01-16
  ```

---

### 4. orders.txt
- **File Path:** `data/orders.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `order_id` (int)
  - `customer_name` (str)
  - `order_date` (str, date YYYY-MM-DD)
  - `total_amount` (float)
  - `status` (str): Order status such as Pending, Shipped, Delivered
  - `shipping_address` (str)
- **Description:** Stores customer orders.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|John Doe|2025-01-10|38.97|Delivered|123 Main St, New York, NY 10001
  2|Jane Smith|2025-01-14|16.99|Shipped|456 Oak Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90001
  ```

---

### 5. order_items.txt
- **File Path:** `data/order_items.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `order_item_id` (int)
  - `order_id` (int)
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `quantity` (int)
  - `price` (float)
- **Description:** Stores items belonging to orders.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|1|1|2|12.99
  2|1|3|1|14.99
  3|2|2|1|16.99
  ```

---

### 6. reviews.txt
- **File Path:** `data/reviews.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `review_id` (int)
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `customer_name` (str)
  - `rating` (int)
  - `review_text` (str)
  - `review_date` (str, date YYYY-MM-DD)
- **Description:** Stores customer reviews.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|5|Amazing book! A true classic.|2025-01-12
  2|2|Bob Williams|4|Very informative and well-written.|2025-01-13
  3|3|Charlie Brown|5|Thought-provoking and relevant today.|2025-01-15
  ```

---

### 7. bestsellers.txt
- **File Path:** `data/bestsellers.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `sales_count` (int)
  - `period` (str): Time period such as "This Week", "This Month", "All Time"
- **Description:** Tracks book sales counts for periods.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  2|150|This Month
  1|120|This Month
  3|95|This Month
  ```

# BookstoreOnline Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification

### 1. Root Route
- **Route Path:** `/`
- **Function Name:** `root_redirect`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Behavior:** Redirects to `/dashboard`

---

### 2. Dashboard Page
- **Route Path:** `/dashboard`
- **Function Name:** `dashboard`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `dashboard.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `featured_books` (list of dict): Each dict representing a book with fields `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `author`(str), `price`(float), `isbn`(str), `category`(str), `description`(str), `stock`(int)
  - `bestsellers_summary` (list of dict): Contains bestsellers data for quick view; fields `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `author`(str), `sales_count`(int)

---

### 3. Book Catalog Page
- **Route Path:** `/catalog`
- **Function Name:** `book_catalog`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `catalog.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `books` (list of dict): Each dict representing a book with fields as in books data
  - `categories` (list of dict): Category dicts containing `category_id`(int), `category_name`(str), `description`(str)
  - `selected_category` (str or None): category name filter value
  - `search_query` (str): search input value

- **Query Parameters:** 
  - `search`: to search books by title, author, or ISBN
  - `category`: to filter by category

- **Form submissions:** None (search/filter handled by query params and GET)

---

### 4. Book Details Page
- **Route Path:** `/book/<int:book_id>`
- **Function Name:** `book_details`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET, POST
- **Template:** `book_details.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `book` (dict): Book details including all book fields
  - `reviews` (list of dict): List of reviews for the book, each review with `review_id`(int), `customer_name`(str), `rating`(int), `review_text`(str), `review_date`(str)

- **POST behavior:**
  - Adding the book to the shopping cart.
  - Form parameters: quantity (default 1, if implemented)
  - On successful add, redirect or reload to same page or cart page.

---

### 5. Shopping Cart Page
- **Route Path:** `/cart`
- **Function Name:** `shopping_cart`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET, POST
- **Template:** `cart.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `cart_items` (list of dict): Each dict representing cart item with `cart_id`(int), `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `quantity`(int), `price`(float), `subtotal`(float)
  - `total_amount` (float): Sum of all cart item subtotals

- **POST behavior:**
  - Handling quantity updates and item removals from cart.
  - Form parameters:
    - For updating quantity: `update_quantity-{item_id}` fields
    - For removing item: triggered by `remove-item-button-{item_id}`
  - After modifications, reload the cart page.

---

### 6. Checkout Page
- **Route Path:** `/checkout`
- **Function Name:** `checkout`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET, POST
- **Template:** `checkout.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `cart_items` (list of dict): As in cart page, current cart items
  - `total_amount` (float): Total cart amount

- **POST behavior:**
  - Handles placing an order
  - Form parameters:
    - `customer_name` (str)
    - `shipping_address` (str)
    - `payment_method` (str): Options "Credit Card", "PayPal", "Bank Transfer"
  - On success: clears cart, stores order and order items data, redirects to order history or confirmation.

---

### 7. Order History Page
- **Route Path:** `/orders`
- **Function Name:** `order_history`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `order_history.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `orders` (list of dict): Each with `order_id`(int), `customer_name`(str), `order_date`(str), `total_amount`(float), `status`(str), `shipping_address`(str)
  - `selected_status` (str): Filter status value (e.g., "All", "Pending", "Shipped", "Delivered")

- **Query Parameters:**
  - `status` to filter orders by their status

---

### 8. Order Details Page [Not explicitly specified but implied for button action]
- **Route Path:** `/order/<int:order_id>`
- **Function Name:** `order_details`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `order_details.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `order` (dict): Order details as above
  - `order_items` (list of dict): Items for this order with fields `order_item_id`(int), `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `quantity`(int), `price`(float)

---

### 9. Reviews Page
- **Route Path:** `/reviews`
- **Function Name:** `reviews_page`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `reviews.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `reviews` (list of dict): Each with all fields as per reviews data plus `book_title`(str)
  - `filter_rating` (str): Current rating filter value

- **Query Parameters:**
  - `rating` to filter reviews by rating

---

### 10. Write Review Page
- **Route Path:** `/write_review`
- **Function Name:** `write_review`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET, POST
- **Template:** `write_review.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `purchased_books` (list of dict): Books purchased by user (minimal info: `book_id`, `title`)

- **POST behavior:**
  - Handles review submission
  - Form parameters:
    - `book_id` (int)
    - `rating` (int)
    - `review_text` (str)
  - On success, add review to file and redirect to reviews page.

---

### 11. Bestsellers Page
- **Route Path:** `/bestsellers`
- **Function Name:** `bestsellers`
- **HTTP Method(s):** GET
- **Template:** `bestsellers.html`
- **Context Variables:**
  - `bestsellers` (list of dict): Each dict with `book_id`(int), `title`(str), `author`(str), `sales_count`(int), `period`(str)
  - `time_period` (str): Filter value (e.g. "This Week", "This Month", "All Time")

- **Query Parameters:**
  - `period` to filter by time period

---

## Section 2: HTML Templates Specification

### 1. templates/dashboard.html
- **Page Title:** Bookstore Dashboard
- **Main Heading (h1):** Bookstore Dashboard
- **Element IDs:**
  - `dashboard-page` (Div): Container for the dashboard page

  - `bestsellers-button` uses `url_for('bestsellers')`

---

### 2. templates/catalog.html

  - `search-input` (Input): Search field by title, author, ISBN
  - `category-filter` (Dropdown): Filter categories
  - `books-grid` (Div): Grid displaying book cards
  - `view-book-button-{book_id}` (Button): Button on each book card

- **Context Variables:**
  - `books` (list of dict)
  - `categories` (list of dict)
  - `selected_category` (str or None)
  - `search_query` (str)

- **Navigation:**
  - `view-book-button-{book_id}` links to `/book/<book_id>` via `url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id)`

---

### 3. templates/book_details.html
- **Page Title:** Book Details
- **Main Heading (h1):** Book Details
- **Element IDs:**
  - `book-details-page` (Div)
  - `book-title` (H1) displays the title
  - `book-author` (Div)
  - `book-price` (Div)
  - `add-to-cart-button` (Button) triggers POST to add to cart
  - `book-reviews` (Div) shows customer reviews

- **Context Variables:**
  - `book` (dict)
  - `reviews` (list of dict)

- **Form Structure:**

  - Quantity can be fixed to 1 or an input field if designed

- **Navigation:**

---

### 4. templates/cart.html
- **Page Title:** Shopping Cart
- **Main Heading (h1):** Shopping Cart
- **Element IDs:**
  - `cart-page` (Div)
  - `cart-items-table` (Table) with rows for each cart item
  - `update-quantity-{item_id}` (Input number) to update quantity
  - `remove-item-button-{item_id}` (Button) to remove item
  - `proceed-checkout-button` (Button) navigates to `/checkout`
  - `total-amount` (Div) displays total price

- **Context Variables:**
  - `cart_items` (list of dict)
  - `total_amount` (float)

- **Form Structure:**
  - Form to update quantities or remove items
  - POST submission to `/cart` route

- **Navigation:**
  - `proceed-checkout-button` uses `url_for('checkout')`

---

### 5. templates/checkout.html
- **Page Title:** Checkout
- **Main Heading (h1):** Checkout
- **Element IDs:**
  - `checkout-page` (Div)
  - `customer-name` (Input)
  - `shipping-address` (Textarea)
  - `payment-method` (Dropdown with "Credit Card", "PayPal", "Bank Transfer")
  - `place-order-button` (Button) submits form

- **Context Variables:**
  - `cart_items` (list of dict)
  - `total_amount` (float)

- **Form Structure:**
  - POST form for placing order

---

### 6. templates/order_history.html
- **Page Title:** Order History
- **Main Heading (h1):** Order History
- **Element IDs:**
  - `orders-page` (Div)
  - `orders-table` (Table) with order rows
  - `view-order-button-{order_id}` (Button) to view details
  - `order-status-filter` (Dropdown) for filtering
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button) navigates to `/dashboard`

- **Context Variables:**
  - `orders` (list of dict)
  - `selected_status` (str)

- **Navigation:**
  - `view-order-button-{order_id}` uses `url_for('order_details', order_id=order_id)`
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

---

### 7. templates/reviews.html
- **Page Title:** Customer Reviews
- **Main Heading (h1):** Customer Reviews
- **Element IDs:**
  - `reviews-page` (Div)
  - `reviews-list` (Div) listing all reviews
  - `write-review-button` (Button) navigates to write review page
  - `filter-by-rating` (Dropdown)
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button) navigates to `/dashboard`

- **Context Variables:**
  - `reviews` (list of dict)
  - `filter_rating` (str)

- **Navigation:**
  - `write-review-button` uses `url_for('write_review')`
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

---

### 8. templates/write_review.html
- **Page Title:** Write a Review
- **Main Heading (h1):** Write a Review
- **Element IDs:**
  - `write-review-page` (Div)
  - `select-book` (Dropdown) to select book
  - `rating-select` (Dropdown) to select rating 1-5 stars
  - `review-text` (Textarea) to enter review
  - `submit-review-button` (Button) submits form

- **Context Variables:**
  - `purchased_books` (list of dict): Books to review

- **Form Structure:**
  - POST form submission to `/write_review`

---

### 9. templates/bestsellers.html
- **Page Title:** Bestsellers
- **Main Heading (h1):** Bestsellers
- **Element IDs:**
  - `bestsellers-page` (Div)
  - `bestsellers-list` (Div) ranked list
  - `time-period-filter` (Dropdown) with options: "This Week", "This Month", "All Time"
  - `view-book-button-{book_id}` (Button) to view book
  - `back-to-dashboard` (Button) navigates to `/dashboard`

- **Context Variables:**
  - `bestsellers` (list of dict)
  - `time_period` (str)

- **Navigation:**
  - `view-book-button-{book_id}` uses `url_for('book_details', book_id=book_id)`
  - `back-to-dashboard` uses `url_for('dashboard')`

---

## Section 3: Data Schemas Specification

### 1. books.txt
- **File Path:** `data/books.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `title` (str)
  - `author` (str)
  - `isbn` (str)
  - `category` (str)
  - `price` (float)
  - `stock` (int)
  - `description` (str)
- **Description:** Stores all book information, including inventory and description.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|The Great Gatsby|F. Scott Fitzgerald|9780743273565|Fiction|12.99|50|A classic American novel
  2|Sapiens|Yuval Noah Harari|9780062316097|Non-Fiction|16.99|30|A brief history of humankind
  3|1984|George Orwell|9780451524935|Fiction|14.99|45|Dystopian social science fiction
  ```

---

### 2. categories.txt
- **File Path:** `data/categories.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `category_id` (int)
  - `category_name` (str)
  - `description` (str)
- **Description:** Stores categories available for books.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|Fiction|Fictional narratives and novels
  2|Non-Fiction|Factual and educational books
  3|Science|Scientific topics and research
  ```

---

### 3. cart.txt
- **File Path:** `data/cart.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `cart_id` (int)
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `quantity` (int)
  - `added_date` (str, date in YYYY-MM-DD)
- **Description:** Stores current cart items (assumed global/cart for single user since no authentication).
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|1|2|2025-01-15
  2|3|1|2025-01-16
  ```

---

### 4. orders.txt
- **File Path:** `data/orders.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `order_id` (int)
  - `customer_name` (str)
  - `order_date` (str, date YYYY-MM-DD)
  - `total_amount` (float)
  - `status` (str): Order status such as Pending, Shipped, Delivered
  - `shipping_address` (str)
- **Description:** Stores customer orders.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|John Doe|2025-01-10|38.97|Delivered|123 Main St, New York, NY 10001
  2|Jane Smith|2025-01-14|16.99|Shipped|456 Oak Ave, Los Angeles, CA 90001
  ```

---

### 5. order_items.txt
- **File Path:** `data/order_items.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `order_item_id` (int)
  - `order_id` (int)
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `quantity` (int)
  - `price` (float)
- **Description:** Stores items belonging to orders.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|1|1|2|12.99
  2|1|3|1|14.99
  3|2|2|1|16.99
  ```

---

### 6. reviews.txt
- **File Path:** `data/reviews.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `review_id` (int)
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `customer_name` (str)
  - `rating` (int)
  - `review_text` (str)
  - `review_date` (str, date YYYY-MM-DD)
- **Description:** Stores customer reviews.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  1|1|Alice Johnson|5|Amazing book! A true classic.|2025-01-12
  2|2|Bob Williams|4|Very informative and well-written.|2025-01-13
  3|3|Charlie Brown|5|Thought-provoking and relevant today.|2025-01-15
  ```

---

### 7. bestsellers.txt
- **File Path:** `data/bestsellers.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `sales_count` (int)
  - `period` (str): Time period such as "This Week", "This Month", "All Time"
- **Description:** Tracks book sales counts for periods.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  2|150|This Month
  1|120|This Month
  3|95|This Month
  ```

  1|1|Alice Johnson|5|Amazing book! A true classic.|2025-01-12
  2|2|Bob Williams|4|Very informative and well-written.|2025-01-13
  3|3|Charlie Brown|5|Thought-provoking and relevant today.|2025-01-15
  ```

---

### 7. bestsellers.txt
- **File Path:** `data/bestsellers.txt`
- **Format:** Pipe-delimited `|`
- **Field Order and Names:**
  - `book_id` (int)
  - `sales_count` (int)
  - `period` (str): Time period such as "This Week"
 "This Month"
 "All Time"
- **Description:** Tracks book sales counts for periods.
- **Example Rows:**
  ```
  2|150|This Month
  1|120|This Month
  3|95|This Month
  ```

log.before:
# JobBoard Web Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification

| Route Path                  | Function Name             | HTTP Methods | Template Rendered       | Context Variables (name: type)                                      | POST Input Expectations                                                                                     |
|-----------------------------|---------------------------|--------------|-------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| /                           | root_redirect             | GET          | N/A (redirect)           | N/A                                                                  | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /dashboard                  | dashboard_page            | GET          | dashboard.html           | featured_jobs: list of dict (fields: job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /jobs                      | job_listings_page         | GET          | job_listings.html        | jobs: list of dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int), categories: list of str, locations: list of str (values: Remote, On-site, Hybrid) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /job/<int:job_id>          | job_details_page          | GET          | job_details.html         | job: dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, description:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int, location:str), company: dict (company_id:int, company_name:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /apply/<int:job_id>        | application_form_page     | GET, POST    | application_form.html    | job: dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str)                 | POST fields: applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), resume (file upload), cover_letter (str)            |
| /applications              | application_tracking_page | GET          | application_tracking.html| applications: list of dict (application_id:int, job_title:str, company_name:str, status:str, applied_date:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /application/<int:application_id> | application_detail_page   | GET          | application_detail.html  | application: dict(application_id:int, job_id:int, applicant_name:str, applicant_email:str, status:str, applied_date:str, resume_id:int), job: dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str), resume: dict (resume_id:int, filename:str, summary:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /companies                 | companies_directory_page  | GET          | companies.html           | companies: list of dict(company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str, employee_count:int) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /company/<int:company_id>  | company_profile_page      | GET          | company_profile.html     | company: dict (company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str, location:str, description:str, employee_count:int), jobs: list of dict (job_id:int, title:str, status:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /resumes                   | resume_management_page    | GET          | resumes.html             | resumes: list of dict (resume_id:int, applicant_name:str, applicant_email:str, filename:str, upload_date:str, summary:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /upload_resume             | upload_resume             | POST         | N/A (redirect or JSON)   | N/A                                                                  | POST fields: applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), resume_file (file upload), summary (str, optional) |
| /delete_resume/<int:resume_id> | delete_resume            | POST         | N/A (redirect)           | N/A                                                                  | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /search                   | search_results_page       | GET          | search_results.html      | query: str, job_results: list of dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int), company_results: list of dict (company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |

Notes:
- Root '/' route redirects to '/dashboard'.
- Application form POST expects form fields with applicant_name, applicant_email, resume file upload, and cover letter text.
- Upload resume POST expects form fields: applicant_name, applicant_email, resume file upload, optionally summary.
- Delete resume is a POST route requiring resume_id as URL parameter.

---

## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications

### 1. Dashboard Page
- Filename: templates/dashboard.html
- Page Title: Job Board Dashboard
- Element IDs:
  - dashboard-page: Div, container for entire dashboard page.
  - featured-jobs: Div, displays featured job recommendations.
  - browse-jobs-button: Button, navigates to job listings page.
  - my-applications-button: Button, navigates to application tracking page.
  - companies-button: Button, navigates to companies directory page.
- Context Variables:
  - featured_jobs: list of dicts, each dict: job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - browse-jobs-button: url_for('job_listings_page')
  - my-applications-button: url_for('application_tracking_page')
  - companies-button: url_for('companies_directory_page')

### 2. Job Listings Page
- Filename: templates/job_listings.html
- Page Title: Job Listings
- Element IDs:
  - listings-page: Div, container for job listings page.
  - search-input: Input, text input for search by title, company, or location.
  - category-filter: Dropdown, select job category.
  - location-filter: Dropdown, select job location (Remote, On-site, Hybrid).
  - jobs-grid: Div, grid layout containing job cards.
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: Button, on each job card to view job details.
- Context Variables:
  - jobs: list of dict, each with job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int)
  - categories: list of strings (category names)
  - locations: list of strings ("Remote", "On-site", "Hybrid")
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details_page', job_id=job.job_id)

### 3. Job Details Page
- Filename: templates/job_details.html
- Page Title: Job Details
- Element IDs:
  - job-details-page: Div, container for the job details page.
  - job-title: H1, displays job title.
  - company-name: Div, displays company name.
  - job-description: Div, displays job description and requirements.
  - salary-range: Div, displays salary range.
  - apply-now-button: Button, navigates to application form page.
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with fields job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), description (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int), location (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - apply-now-button: url_for('application_form_page', job_id=job.job_id)

### 4. Application Form Page
- Filename: templates/application_form.html
- Page Title: Submit Application
- Element IDs:
  - application-form-page: Div, container for the application form.
  - applicant-name: Input, text input for applicant name.
  - applicant-email: Input, text input for applicant email.
  - resume-upload: File Input, upload resume file.
  - cover-letter: Textarea, input for cover letter text.
  - submit-application-button: Button, submits the application form.
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with fields job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - submit-application-button: Submits form POST to the same route.

### 5. Application Tracking Page
- Filename: templates/application_tracking.html
- Page Title: My Applications
- Element IDs:
  - tracking-page: Div, container for the tracking page.
  - applications-table: Table, displays applications with columns: Job Title, Company, Status, Date Applied.
  - status-filter: Dropdown, filter applications by status (All, Applied, Under Review, Interview, Rejected).
  - view-application-button-{{ application.application_id }}: Button, view details of each application.
  - back-to-dashboard: Button, navigates back to dashboard.
- Context Variables:
  - applications: list of dicts, each with application_id (int), job_title (str), company_name (str), status (str), applied_date (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-application-button-{{ application.application_id }}: url_for('application_detail_page', application_id=application.application_id)
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_page')

### 6. Companies Directory Page
- Filename: templates/companies.html
- Page Title: Company Directory
- Element IDs:
  - companies-page: Div, container for the companies directory.
  - companies-list: Div, list of company cards.
  - search-company-input: Input, search companies by name or industry.
  - view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }}: Button, view company profile.
  - back-to-dashboard: Button, navigates back to dashboard.
- Context Variables:
  - companies: list of dicts with company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str), employee_count (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }}: url_for('company_profile_page', company_id=company.company_id)
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_page')

### 7. Company Profile Page
- Filename: templates/company_profile.html
- Page Title: Company Profile
- Element IDs:
  - company-profile-page: Div, container for the company profile.
  - company-info: Div, displays company name, industry, location, description.
  - company-jobs: Div, displays all open jobs for the company.
  - jobs-list: Div, list of job cards with titles and status indicators.
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: Button, view job details.
  - back-to-companies: Button, navigates back to companies directory.
- Context Variables:
  - company: dict(company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str, location:str, description:str, employee_count:int)
  - jobs: list of dict(job_id:int, title:str, status:str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details_page', job_id=job.job_id)
  - back-to-companies: url_for('companies_directory_page')

### 8. Resume Management Page
- Filename: templates/resumes.html
- Page Title: My Resumes
- Element IDs:
  - resume-page: Div, container for resume management page.
  - resumes-list: Div, list of uploaded resumes with upload date.
  - upload-resume-button: Button, triggers file input.
  - resume-file-input: File Input (hidden), file input for uploading resumes.
  - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: Button, deletes a resume.
  - back-to-dashboard: Button, navigates back to dashboard.
- Context Variables:
  - resumes: list of dict(resume_id:int, applicant_name:str, applicant_email:str, filename:str, upload_date:str, summary:str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - upload-resume-button: triggers click of resume-file-input
  - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: POST to /delete_resume/{{ resume.resume_id }}
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_page')

### 9. Search Results Page
- Filename: templates/search_results.html
- Page Title: Search Results
- Element IDs:
  - search-results-page: Div, container for the search results page.
  - search-query-display: Div, shows user's search query.
  - results-tabs: Div, tabs to switch between job and company results.
  - job-results: Div, container for job search results.
  - company-results: Div, container for company search results.
  - no-results-message: Div, shown when no results found.
- Context Variables:
  - query: string, the search query text.
  - job_results: list of dict(job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int)
  - company_results: list of dict(company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view job details and company profiles via buttons with appropriate url_for following job and company route conventions

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas

### 1. jobs.txt
- Path: data/jobs.txt
- Fields: job_id|title|company_id|location|salary_min|salary_max|category|description|posted_date
- Description: Stores job postings with detailed information including company id linkage.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15
  - 2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16
  - 3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14

### 2. companies.txt
- Path: data/companies.txt
- Fields: company_id|company_name|industry|location|employee_count|description
- Description: Stores company profiles including industry and size.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider
  - 2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company
  - 3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization

### 3. categories.txt
- Path: data/categories.txt
- Fields: category_id|category_name|description
- Description: Stores job category details.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions
  - 2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions
  - 3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions

### 4. applications.txt
- Path: data/applications.txt
- Fields: application_id|job_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|status|applied_date|resume_id
- Description: Stores job applications with status and linkage to resumes.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1
  - 2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2
  - 3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3

### 5. resumes.txt
- Path: data/resumes.txt
- Fields: resume_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|filename|upload_date|summary
- Description: Stores uploaded resumes with metadata.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience
  - 2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background
  - 3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience

### 6. job_categories.txt
- Path: data/job_categories.txt
- Fields: mapping_id|job_id|category_id
- Description: Maps jobs to categories.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|1|1
  - 2|2|2
  - 3|3|3

---

This comprehensive design spec enables backend and frontend developers to implement the 'JobBoard' Flask application with full consistency and completeness.

 input for uploading resumes.

## Section 3: Data File Schemas
### 1. jobs.txt
- Path: './data/jobs.data'

- Fields: job_id,title,company_id,location,salary_min,salary_max,category,description,posted_date

- Description: Stores job postings without company id linkage.

- Example Rows:
  - 1;Senior Python Developer;1;Remote;80k;120k;Tech;Experienced Python developer;2025-01-15
  - 2;Data Analyst;2;NY;60000;85000;Finance;Analyze financial data;2025-01-16
  - 3;Healthcare Administrator;3;LA;50000;70000;Healthcare;Manage hospital;2025-01-14
### 2. companies.txt
- Path: './data/companies.data'
- Fields: company_id,company_name,industry,location,employee_count,description
- Description: Stores profiles without industry info.

- Description: Stores job categories.

- Fields: app_id,job_id,applicant_name,applicant_email,status,applied_date,resume_id
- Description: Stores applications status and resume links.
- Example Rows:
  - 1;1;John Doe;john@email.com;Review;2025-01-17;1

  - 3;3;Robert Johnson;robert@email.com;Interview;2025-01-15;3

- Path: data/resumes.txt
- Fields: resume_id,applicant_name,applicant_email,filename,upload_date,summary
- Description: Stores resume metadata.

  - 2;Jane Smith;jane@email.com;jane_resume.pdf;2025-01-16;Data analyst finance
  - 3;Robert Johnson;robert@email.com;robert_resume.pdf;2025-01-15;Healthcare admin 5 yrs

# JobBoard Web Application Design Specification

---

## Section 1: Flask Routes Specification

| Route Path                  | Function Name             | HTTP Methods | Template Rendered       | Context Variables (name: type)                                      | POST Input Expectations                                                                                     |
|-----------------------------|---------------------------|--------------|-------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

| /dashboard                  | dashboard_page            | GET          | dashboard.html           | featured_jobs: list of dict (fields: job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /jobs                      | job_listings_page         | GET          | job_listings.html        | jobs: list of dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int), categories: list of str, locations: list of str (values: Remote, On-site, Hybrid) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /job/<int:job_id>          | job_details_page          | GET          | job_details.html         | job: dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, description:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int, location:str), company: dict (company_id:int, company_name:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /apply/<int:job_id>        | application_form_page     | GET, POST    | application_form.html    | job: dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str)                 | POST fields: applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), resume (file upload), cover_letter (str)            |
| /applications              | application_tracking_page | GET          | application_tracking.html| applications: list of dict (application_id:int, job_title:str, company_name:str, status:str, applied_date:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /application/<int:application_id> | application_detail_page   | GET          | application_detail.html  | application: dict(application_id:int, job_id:int, applicant_name:str, applicant_email:str, status:str, applied_date:str, resume_id:int), job: dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str), resume: dict (resume_id:int, filename:str, summary:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /companies                 | companies_directory_page  | GET          | companies.html           | companies: list of dict(company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str, employee_count:int) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /company/<int:company_id>  | company_profile_page      | GET          | company_profile.html     | company: dict (company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str, location:str, description:str, employee_count:int), jobs: list of dict (job_id:int, title:str, status:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /resumes                   | resume_management_page    | GET          | resumes.html             | resumes: list of dict (resume_id:int, applicant_name:str, applicant_email:str, filename:str, upload_date:str, summary:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /upload_resume             | upload_resume             | POST         | N/A (redirect or JSON)   | N/A                                                                  | POST fields: applicant_name (str), applicant_email (str), resume_file (file upload), summary (str, optional) |
| /delete_resume/<int:resume_id> | delete_resume            | POST         | N/A (redirect)           | N/A                                                                  | N/A                                                                                                         |
| /search                   | search_results_page       | GET          | search_results.html      | query: str, job_results: list of dict (job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int), company_results: list of dict (company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str) | N/A                                                                                                         |

Notes:
- Root '/' route redirects to '/dashboard'.
- Application form POST expects form fields with applicant_name, applicant_email, resume file upload, and cover letter text.
- Upload resume POST expects form fields: applicant_name, applicant_email, resume file upload, optionally summary.
- Delete resume is a POST route requiring resume_id as URL parameter.

---

## Section 2: HTML Template Specifications

### 1. Dashboard Page
- Filename: templates/dashboard.html
- Page Title: Job Board Dashboard
- Element IDs:
  - dashboard-page: Div, container for entire dashboard page.
  - featured-jobs: Div, displays featured job recommendations.
  - browse-jobs-button: Button, navigates to job listings page.
  - my-applications-button: Button, navigates to application tracking page.
  - companies-button: Button, navigates to companies directory page.
- Context Variables:
  - featured_jobs: list of dicts, each dict: job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - browse-jobs-button: url_for('job_listings_page')
  - my-applications-button: url_for('application_tracking_page')
  - companies-button: url_for('companies_directory_page')

### 2. Job Listings Page
- Filename: templates/job_listings.html
- Page Title: Job Listings
- Element IDs:
  - listings-page: Div, container for job listings page.
  - search-input: Input, text input for search by title, company, or location.
  - category-filter: Dropdown, select job category.
  - location-filter: Dropdown, select job location (Remote, On-site, Hybrid).
  - jobs-grid: Div, grid layout containing job cards.
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: Button, on each job card to view job details.
- Context Variables:
  - jobs: list of dict, each with job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), location (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int)
  - categories: list of strings (category names)
  - locations: list of strings ("Remote", "On-site", "Hybrid")
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details_page', job_id=job.job_id)

### 3. Job Details Page
- Filename: templates/job_details.html
- Page Title: Job Details
- Element IDs:
  - job-details-page: Div, container for the job details page.
  - job-title: H1, displays job title.
  - company-name: Div, displays company name.
  - job-description: Div, displays job description and requirements.
  - salary-range: Div, displays salary range.
  - apply-now-button: Button, navigates to application form page.
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with fields job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str), description (str), salary_min (int), salary_max (int), location (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - apply-now-button: url_for('application_form_page', job_id=job.job_id)

### 4. Application Form Page
- Filename: templates/application_form.html
- Page Title: Submit Application
- Element IDs:
  - application-form-page: Div, container for the application form.
  - applicant-name: Input, text input for applicant name.
  - applicant-email: Input, text input for applicant email.
  - resume-upload: File Input, upload resume file.
  - cover-letter: Textarea, input for cover letter text.
  - submit-application-button: Button, submits the application form.
- Context Variables:
  - job: dict with fields job_id (int), title (str), company_name (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - submit-application-button: Submits form POST to the same route.

### 5. Application Tracking Page
- Filename: templates/application_tracking.html
- Page Title: My Applications
- Element IDs:
  - tracking-page: Div, container for the tracking page.
  - applications-table: Table, displays applications with columns: Job Title, Company, Status, Date Applied.
  - status-filter: Dropdown, filter applications by status (All, Applied, Under Review, Interview, Rejected).
  - view-application-button-{{ application.application_id }}: Button, view details of each application.
  - back-to-dashboard: Button, navigates back to dashboard.
- Context Variables:
  - applications: list of dicts, each with application_id (int), job_title (str), company_name (str), status (str), applied_date (str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-application-button-{{ application.application_id }}: url_for('application_detail_page', application_id=application.application_id)
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_page')

### 6. Companies Directory Page
- Filename: templates/companies.html
- Page Title: Company Directory
- Element IDs:
  - companies-page: Div, container for the companies directory.
  - companies-list: Div, list of company cards.
  - search-company-input: Input, search companies by name or industry.
  - view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }}: Button, view company profile.
  - back-to-dashboard: Button, navigates back to dashboard.
- Context Variables:
  - companies: list of dicts with company_id (int), company_name (str), industry (str), employee_count (int)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-company-button-{{ company.company_id }}: url_for('company_profile_page', company_id=company.company_id)
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_page')

### 7. Company Profile Page
- Filename: templates/company_profile.html
- Page Title: Company Profile
- Element IDs:
  - company-profile-page: Div, container for the company profile.
  - company-info: Div, displays company name, industry, location, description.
  - company-jobs: Div, displays all open jobs for the company.
  - jobs-list: Div, list of job cards with titles and status indicators.
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: Button, view job details.
  - back-to-companies: Button, navigates back to companies directory.
- Context Variables:
  - company: dict(company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str, location:str, description:str, employee_count:int)
  - jobs: list of dict(job_id:int, title:str, status:str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view-job-button-{{ job.job_id }}: url_for('job_details_page', job_id=job.job_id)
  - back-to-companies: url_for('companies_directory_page')

### 8. Resume Management Page
- Filename: templates/resumes.html
- Page Title: My Resumes
- Element IDs:
  - resume-page: Div, container for resume management page.
  - resumes-list: Div, list of uploaded resumes with upload date.
  - upload-resume-button: Button, triggers file input.
  - resume-file-input: File Input (hidden), file input for uploading resumes.
  - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: Button, deletes a resume.
  - back-to-dashboard: Button, navigates back to dashboard.
- Context Variables:
  - resumes: list of dict(resume_id:int, applicant_name:str, applicant_email:str, filename:str, upload_date:str, summary:str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - upload-resume-button: triggers click of resume-file-input
  - delete-resume-button-{{ resume.resume_id }}: POST to /delete_resume/{{ resume.resume_id }}
  - back-to-dashboard: url_for('dashboard_page')

### 9. Search Results Page
- Filename: templates/search_results.html
- Page Title: Search Results
- Element IDs:
  - search-results-page: Div, container for the search results page.
  - search-query-display: Div, shows user's search query.
  - results-tabs: Div, tabs to switch between job and company results.
  - job-results: Div, container for job search results.
  - company-results: Div, container for company search results.
  - no-results-message: Div, shown when no results found.
- Context Variables:
  - query: string, the search query text.
  - job_results: list of dict(job_id:int, title:str, company_name:str, location:str, salary_min:int, salary_max:int)
  - company_results: list of dict(company_id:int, company_name:str, industry:str)
- Navigation Mappings:
  - view job details and company profiles via buttons with appropriate url_for following job and company route conventions

---

## Section 3: Data File Schemas

### 1. jobs.txt
- Path: data/jobs.txt
- Fields: job_id|title|company_id|location|salary_min|salary_max|category|description|posted_date
- Description: Stores job postings with detailed information including company id linkage.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|Senior Python Developer|1|Remote|80000|120000|Technology|Experienced Python developer for web applications|2025-01-15
  - 2|Data Analyst|2|New York, NY|60000|85000|Finance|Analyze financial data and create reports|2025-01-16
  - 3|Healthcare Administrator|3|Los Angeles, CA|50000|70000|Healthcare|Manage hospital operations and patient records|2025-01-14

### 2. companies.txt
- Path: data/companies.txt
- Fields: company_id|company_name|industry|location|employee_count|description
- Description: Stores company profiles including industry and size.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|TechCorp|Technology|San Francisco, CA|500|Leading software solutions provider
  - 2|FinanceHub|Finance|New York, NY|300|Innovative financial services company
  - 3|MediCare|Healthcare|Los Angeles, CA|200|Premier healthcare management organization

### 3. categories.txt
- Path: data/categories.txt
- Fields: category_id|category_name|description
- Description: Stores job category details.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|Technology|Software, IT, and tech-related positions
  - 2|Finance|Banking, accounting, and finance positions
  - 3|Healthcare|Medical and healthcare industry positions

### 4. applications.txt
- Path: data/applications.txt
- Fields: application_id|job_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|status|applied_date|resume_id
- Description: Stores job applications with status and linkage to resumes.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|1|John Doe|john@email.com|Under Review|2025-01-17|1
  - 2|2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|Applied|2025-01-16|2
  - 3|3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|Interview|2025-01-15|3

### 5. resumes.txt
- Path: data/resumes.txt
- Fields: resume_id|applicant_name|applicant_email|filename|upload_date|summary
- Description: Stores uploaded resumes with metadata.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|John Doe|john@email.com|john_resume.pdf|2025-01-17|Senior developer with 8 years experience
  - 2|Jane Smith|jane@email.com|jane_resume.pdf|2025-01-16|Data analyst with financial background
  - 3|Robert Johnson|robert@email.com|robert_resume.pdf|2025-01-15|Healthcare administrator with 5 years experience

### 6. job_categories.txt
- Path: data/job_categories.txt
- Fields: mapping_id|job_id|category_id
- Description: Maps jobs to categories.
- Example Rows:
  - 1|1|1
  - 2|2|2
  - 3|3|3
